c1-console.cluster: c1-console — Console broker signal anchoring: record must anchor to the held group; broker forwards TERM and cleans up; plus attach EAGAIN drop and post-EOF waitpid hang

play_verdicts.play: Anchor console-call records to the held process group via the existing target_pid_pipe handshake (PBX 'attendant is never the addressed party')
play_verdicts.reason: The finding is tool-verified FACT and I re-verified every citation: start.c:1293-1295 records r.pid=r.pgid=r.sid = the broker; the console branch is excluded from the pipe at start.c:1017; broker.c installs only SIGPIPE-ignore (broker.c:293-299); signal.c:263 kill(-r.pgid) therefore TERMs the broker, skipping broker_cleanup_and_exit and hold_mark_run_finished. The play's core claims all check out: the target does setsid+TIOCSCTTY (broker.c:205-216) so pid==pgid==sid holds exactly as the record shape and validate_signal_target (signal.c:130-136, getpgid/getsid cross-check) require; the parent reader at start.c:1241-1261 is mode-agnostic; identity tokens at start.c:1389-1390 are read after exec-handshake success so they describe the real executable; the adopted path already anchors to the adopted group (shell.c:538-540, 558-559), so this makes all three modes consistent; console+restart is rejected at start.c:823-825, so flipping the gate to '!restart_enabled' is exactly 'console or plain non-restart'. I also found the play UNDERSELLS itself: today's rollback paths (start.c:1424/1452/1532 kill only the broker's group) orphan a live console target on record-write failure — post-play hold_rollback_spawned_group(pid,pid) kills the real target group and kill_supervisor_if_distinct (start.c:98-106) reaps the broker, with console_sock explicitly unlinked at 1430/1458/1536. Grep of all 4,228 lines of test_hold.sh confirms assumption 3: no test ends a console call with assertions or pins the broker-anchored pid — the bug ships silently through 135/135. Three claims in the play are wrong and are corrected in amendments: (1) 'end then list can briefly show running' is false — hold_eval_state (state.c:39-47) reports STATE_EXITED off the zombie; the real transient is 'Exited (0)' before the broker's mark lands with 143; (2) 'adopted entry passes -1' is moot — hold_run_console_broker_adopted calls broker_serve directly (broker.c:269-271), never hold_run_console_broker, so no parameter reaches it; (3) the strongest objection ('unkillable broker, socket unlink on goodwill only') is overstated — purge already unlinks console_sock (list.c:645,705), and after this play `hold end` unblocks even a waitpid-wedged broker by killing its child. The 'optional' SIGTERM forwarder is NOT optional: once the record stops naming the broker, a raw kill of the broker silently orphans the target; the cluster contract requires forward+cleanup.
amendments: A1 (ordering, prevents parent deadlock at start.c:1243): in hold_run_console_broker, write the target pid to the new fd immediately after exec-handshake success (broker.c:247-249) and BEFORE close(parent_pipe) at broker.c:250; treat a failed pid write as broker_fail_errno (kills target via broker_cleanup_and_exit, unlinks socket, reports errno while parent_pipe is still open). Close the fd in the fail helpers before the write point as hygiene (_exit closes it anyway, so no deadlock exists on those paths — parent takes the handshake>0 branch at start.c:1220 and closes its read end at 1234).

A2 (signature scope): add `int target_pid_fd` to hold_run_console_broker only (console.h:18-30); broker_serve and hold_run_console_broker_adopted are untouched — the dossier's 'adopted entry passes -1' is a mischaracterization; nothing adopted-side changes.

A3 (make the TERM forwarder mandatory, child-mode only): volatile pid_t g_forward_target set to the forked target right after the pid write; sigaction(SIGTERM, handler, no SA_RESTART) installed in hold_run_console_broker only (never in the adopted entry — broker.c:273-275 contract: never signal an adopted group); the handler itself calls kill(-t, SIGTERM) (async-signal-safe), which also unblocks the post-loop blocking waitpid at broker.c:424 if TERM arrives during that window; clear g_forward_target after each reap site.

A4 (ACCESS corner): with target-anchored state, a crashed broker + live target now yields state RUNNING and a silent rc-3 attach (hold_run_native_console returns 3 with no message, attach.c:96-98; commands.c:18-26 only handles st!=RUNNING). Add one stderr line on connect failure ('hold: cannot attach: <strerror>') so this corner is not silent — today's path at least printed a (dishonest) 'has ended' note.

A5 (test discipline): all new end-console assertions must poll the record (record_ended_soon at test_hold.sh:521 + exit_code/term_signal greps, the test_console_exit_code_is_recorded idiom at 1986-1991), never grep `list` immediately — the zombie-to-mark window renders 'Exited (0)' transiently.

A6 (scope): carve the EAGAIN and post-EOF fixes into separate commits (they are cluster findings, not part of this play's diff); commit 1 is anchoring + TERM forwarder + attach note + tests only, ~40 lines of product code.

play_verdicts.play: Finding-2 remedy as de-facto play: stop treating EAGAIN on the nonblocking attach client as fatal (broker.c:370, 410 via hold_write_all, util.c:41-59)
play_verdicts.reason: Finding verified FACT: set_fd_nonblocking at broker.c:362, hold_write_all returns -1 on EAGAIN (util.c:44-50 handles only EINTR), and both the 64KB replay write (broker.c:370) and the live stream (broker.c:410-414) drop the client on it. But the remedy hint's first option (poll POLLOUT, even bounded) is wrong for this tool: blocking the serve loop on a slow viewer stalls log writes and backpressures the PTY into the held call — a viewer must never be able to freeze the call the broker exists to protect (hold-on-identity.md: the broker holds the PTY alive). The correct minimal fix is a bounded per-client output backlog (cap = CONSOLE_REPLAY_LIMIT, 64KB, console_internal.h:19): try-write, stash the remainder, flush on POLLOUT, drop only past the cap. This guarantees the initial replay can never fail at accept time (backlog cap == replay cap) — which is also the macOS/BSD small-sndbuf fix — and converts 'dropped on first EAGAIN' into 'dropped only after 64KB of sustained lag'. The remedy's second half (attach client reports an abnormal drop instead of rc 0) is structurally impossible in the current protocol: the server-to-client stream is raw terminal bytes (broker.c:410), messages exist only pre-stream ('denied'/'already attached'), and a full socket cannot carry a farewell anyway — that half requires server-to-client framing, a protocol version bump. Deferred by name.
amendments: Route BOTH broker.c:370 (replay) and broker.c:410 (live bytes) through one static broker_client_send/broker_client_flush pair; hold_write_all may no longer touch the client fd; EINTR retried, EAGAIN buffered, hard errors still drop.
Preserve the pinned ordering authorize -> replay -> stream (test_hold.sh:2185-2233 pins deny-before-replay; 2235-2270 pins replay content on reattach).

Determinism hook: under HOLD_TESTING only (precedent: HOLD_TEST_FAIL_RECORD_WRITE at start.c:1406), honor HOLD_TEST_CONSOLE_SNDBUF via setsockopt(SO_SNDBUF) on the accepted client so the EAGAIN path is testable without 200KB+ kernel-buffer races.
DEFER-attach-drop-visibility: client-side abnormal-drop reporting (protocol v2 framing) — named deferral, own design pass.

play_verdicts.play: Finding-3 remedy as de-facto play: do not block in waitpid with the listener open after PTY EOF (broker.c:415-417, 421-437)
play_verdicts.reason: Finding verified FACT: on master EOF the loop breaks and the broker blocks in waitpid while the listener socket stays connectable, so attach hangs raw-mode-blind forever; targets that daemonize their stdio are exactly hold's clientele. Two corrections to severity ordering, both caused by the anchor play: (a) it makes recovery BETTER, not worse — post-anchor, `hold end` TERMs the target group, waitpid returns, the broker marks the true status and unlinks the socket, so the wedge is no longer a dead end; the play's own 'schedule it next' answer is therefore acceptable, but the attach-hangs-forever UX remains and must land in this cluster; (b) commit 1's in-handler kill(-target) already covers TERM-during-blocked-waitpid, so this commit is purely about attach responsiveness. Fix shape: on master EOF in child mode, do not break — close master, mark it gone, keep polling listener (1s tick already drives the WNOHANG reap at broker.c:311-321); accepted clients get authorize -> replay -> one-line notice ('console closed - call still running, see hold logs') -> close, mirroring the existing 'already attached' pre-stream message pattern (broker.c:367-369); break when target_done. Adopted mode keeps the EOF break unchanged (its exit detection IS the EOF, and the hup_pid HUP at broker.c:438-440 must fire).
amendments: Only the !adopted path changes; adopted EOF semantics and the hup_pid HUP are pinned by the shell-adoption tests and the broker.c:273-275 contract.

Break immediately when target_done && master_gone (avoid a stray 1s tail); keep the post-loop blocking waitpid for the rare poll-error break path — with the commit-1 TERM handler it can no longer wedge unrecoverably.
Close any currently-attached client at master EOF (matches today's observable: their stream ends), so the only new behavior is that NEW attaches get an honest notice instead of an infinite hang.

pinned_conflicts.what: Pins the broker's hold_mark_run_finished path: exit_code 7, state exited, ps -a 'Exited (7)', prune. Must pass UNMODIFIED after every commit — it is the canary that target-anchoring did not break the broker's reap/mark (broker.c:311-321).

Pins 'Up' in list for a live console call (line 1945) and socket absence after prune (1954-1959). 'Up' is now derived from the TARGET's liveness instead of the broker's — passes, but the meaning under the assertion changes; re-verify, do not edit.

Hard-asserts `hold stop <id>` rc 0 on a console call (`|| return 1`). Today it passes by TERMing the broker (target dies of PTY EOF); post-change it passes by TERMing the real group. Green-to-green with different mechanics — must re-run; any flake here means the escalation/wait window regressed.
Same hard-asserted stop on a console call whose target is `sh` + `sleep 30` in one group; post-change both die by group TERM instead of the sleep being orphaned for 30s.
Pins authorize-before-replay and zero replay leakage to an unauthorized peer. Commit 3's client-send refactor must keep the exact accept-path ordering at broker.c:359-377.
Pins replay-buffer content and ordering on a second attach ('seen:first' replayed before live data). Commit 3 routes the replay write through the new bounded sender — byte order must be preserved.

pinned_conflicts.where: tests/test_hold.sh (whole file, verified by grep) + tests/test_040_dockerish.sh (no tty/console coverage)
pinned_conflicts.what: NO test anywhere ends/kills a console call with assertions, pins the console record's pid/pgid/sid to the broker, or asserts console --print output — the critical bug is entirely unpinned. The play's assumption 3 is confirmed; the new tests below become the pin.

implementation_risks: 1. Parent deadlock at start.c:1243: the parent's target_pid_pipe read has no timeout; if the broker ever closes parent_pipe before writing the pid and then wedges, `hold -t` hangs forever. Enforced ordering (write pid -> close pid fd -> close parent_pipe) makes EOF-on-handshake imply pid-already-written-or-broker-dead; pid-write failure must route through broker_fail_errno so the target is killed and the socket unlinked.

2. Rollback half-states: on short pid read (start.c:1246-1259) `pid` is still the broker, so hold_rollback_spawned_group kills the broker's group but NOT the target (different session since broker.c:205); the target leaks only if the broker hard-crashed (its own fail paths SIGKILL the target at broker.c:70-75). On record-write failure (start.c:1530-1542) post-play: target group killed directly, broker SIGKILLed by kill_supervisor_if_distinct (start.c:98-106) — waitpid on the non-child target returns ECHILD immediately (no hang), and the explicit unlink(console_sock) at 1536-1538 covers the socket. Verify both paths by hand; do not 'fix' them further in this commit.

3. Exit-status transient: between group death and the broker's hold_mark_run_finished, hold_eval_state returns STATE_EXITED off the zombie (state.c:39-47) with no exit_code, so list renders 'Exited (0)' for a beat before 'Exited (143)'. Tests must poll the record; the play's risk text misdiagnoses this window as 'shows running'.

4. TERM-forwarder correctness: handler must be installed only in the child-mode entry (adopted groups are never signaled, broker.c:273-275); no SA_RESTART so poll/waitpid EINTR; kill() from the handler is async-signal-safe and covers the blocked-waitpid window; clear the forward target after reap to shrink the recycled-pgid race to microseconds.

5. Commit-3 accounting bugs: partial-write offset math, POLLOUT registration lifetime, overflow-drop threshold, and freeing the backlog on every client-drop path — the classic ring-buffer defect class. Keep it a flat 64KB buffer with head/len, not a second ring; cap == CONSOLE_REPLAY_LIMIT so the replay always fits.

6. Commit-2 loop-exit matrix: adopted vs child, master EOF with a grandchild still holding the slave (master stays live after target reap — keep today's break-on-target_done-at-tick), and immediate break on target_done && master_gone. Getting one arm wrong either hangs the broker or kills consoles with live grandchildren.

7. Platform divergence: pipe2/O_CLOEXEC fallback already patterned at start.c:1018-1037 (nothing new needed); TIOCSCTTY is ifdef'd (broker.c:210); macOS group liveness is a sysctl scan (platform/process.c:111+) — the anchor works identically because the target setsids on both. The SO_SNDBUF test hook must be HOLD_TESTING-gated so the shipping binary has zero test surface.

8. Cross-cluster surface contention: start.c (gate at 1017, call site at 1157), broker.c, and signal.c are named shared surfaces for other clusters; land commit 1 first and small so rebases against record.c/start.c changes stay trivial. The console.h signature change forces recompilation of all console callers — there is exactly one (start.c:1157), verified by grep.

9. Zero-warning discipline: the new broker parameter must be consumed on every path (no (void) shims left behind), and the sig handler must not trip -Wunused or strict-prototypes; run `make` warning-clean plus scripts/lint_layers.sh (no new includes cross layers: broker.c already includes store.h for hold_mark_run_finished; start.c passes a bare int fd).
c1-console.revised_change_set:
Three commits on tempo-refinement, each independently green (make zero-warning, full test_hold.sh, scripts/lint_layers.sh).

COMMIT 1 — 'console: anchor call records to the held group; broker forwards TERM' (closes critical #1).
Files: include/hold/console.h, src/console/broker.c, src/runtime/start.c, src/console/attach.c, tests/test_hold.sh.
Sequence: (1) console.h: add `int target_pid_fd` as the second parameter of hold_run_console_broker with a comment pinning the contract: '-1 = do not report; otherwise the broker writes its forked target pid exactly once after exec-handshake success, then closes the fd; the write precedes closing parent_pipe'. (2) broker.c hold_run_console_broker: after the handshake-success check (current lines 244-249) and BEFORE close(parent_pipe) at 250: if target_pid_fd >= 0 { if (hold_write_all(target_pid_fd, &target, sizeof(target)) != 0) broker_fail_errno(...); close(target_pid_fd); } — parent_pipe is still open there so the errno report reaches the parent's handshake read. Close target_pid_fd at the top of broker_fail_errno's callers before that point (hygiene; _exit covers it). Then set static volatile pid_t g_broker_forward_target = target and install a SIGTERM sigaction (handler: pid_t t = g_broker_forward_target; if (t > 1) kill(-t, SIGTERM); — async-signal-safe; no SA_RESTART) before calling broker_serve. Clear g_broker_forward_target after both reap sites (broker_serve WNOHANG block ~311-321 and post-loop ~427-433; adopted brokers never set it, so the shared clear is a no-op there). Adopted entry (hold_run_console_broker_adopted) untouched. (3) start.c: flip line 1017 to `if (!restart_enabled)`; pass target_pid_pipe[1] at the 1157 call site. No other parent changes: the reader at 1241-1261 re-anchors pid, the record block at 1285-1295 and identity reads at 1389-1390 then describe the target; supervisor_pid keeps naming the broker for the existing rollback helpers. (4) attach.c hold_run_native_console: on hold_connect_console_socket failure, print one stderr line 'hold: cannot attach: <strerror(errno)>' before returning (covers the broker-crashed/live-target corner that target-anchored state now exposes). (5) Tests: add test_end_console_call_signals_target (and optionally the KILL-escalation twin) per the test plan.

COMMIT 2 — 'console: keep serving after PTY EOF while the target runs' (closes high #3).
Files: src/console/broker.c, tests/test_hold.sh.
broker_serve, !adopted only: at master EOF/EIO (current 415-417) set master_gone, close(master), master = -1, and drop it from the pollfd set instead of breaking; close any attached client; on accept while master_gone: authorize -> hold_console_replay_write -> write 'hold: console closed - call still running, see hold logs\r\n' -> close (mirrors the 'already attached' pre-stream message at 367-369); break when target_done && master_gone immediately, and keep the existing pr==0/target_done break. Adopted path unchanged (EOF still breaks; hup_pid HUP at 438-440 preserved). The post-loop blocking waitpid remains only for the poll-error break path and is no longer reachable with a live listener as the sole exit.

COMMIT 3 — 'console: bounded per-client output backlog; EAGAIN is not fatal' (closes high #2).
Files: src/console/broker.c, tests/test_hold.sh.
Add a flat per-client 64KB backlog (cap = CONSOLE_REPLAY_LIMIT) with static broker_client_send(client_state, buf, n) and broker_client_flush(): try write(2) directly when backlog empty; on EAGAIN/partial, stash the remainder; request POLLOUT on the client while backlog nonempty; flush on POLLOUT; drop the client (close + free backlog) only when a send would exceed the cap; EINTR retried; other errno still drops. Route the initial replay (line 370) and the live master bytes (line 410) through it; hold_write_all no longer touches the client fd. Under #ifdef HOLD_TESTING honor HOLD_TEST_CONSOLE_SNDBUF via setsockopt(SO_SNDBUF) on the accepted client. Preserve the accept-path order authorize -> replay -> stream exactly.

NAMED DEFERRALS (do not fold in): DEFER-attach-drop-visibility (server-to-client control framing so `hold attach` can exit nonzero on an abnormal drop — protocol v2, needs its own design; the raw byte stream cannot carry a farewell today); DEFER-adopted-term-cleanup (TERM handling for adopted brokers; purge already unlinks leaked sockets via list.c:645,705); DEFER-wedged-broker-verb (any hold-verb addressing the broker directly — deliberately out, the record no longer names it). Dossier findings #4-#8 (console.md rewrite, hold on/off coverage, Ctrl-P flush, shell_id_available sock probe, detach-key matcher) are other-cluster work and must not ride these commits.

The trap-HUP guard is the discriminator: under today's code the group survives the broker's death and the record fabricates Exited (0) — this test FAILS on current HEAD and pins the fix. c2-endings.cluster: C2 — Honest endings: restart supervisor final frame, loader gate for Exited (?), foreground exit-code propagation, -e/--env self-pollution

play_verdicts.play: FDR play (dossier): restart supervisor writes the final frame (start.c:288-372) + loader stops laundering State.ExitCode into has_exit_code for non-exited records (record.c:658-665), unblocking the honest 'Exited (?)' at list.c:135
play_verdicts.reason: Both findings re-verified against source: run_restart_supervisor ends with _exit(0) at start.c:371 and never marks; the waiter that marks (start.c:1106-1117) is gated off for restart because target_pid_pipe is created only when '!console_mode && !restart_enabled' (start.c:1017). The writer bakes ExitCode 0 into every running record (record.c:97) and the loader trusts it back only via the State-object FALLBACK (record.c:661-665) — the lowercase 'exit_code' key (written only when has_exit_code, record.c:261-262) is the primary path, so the gate is surgical: it only affects records hold wrote while running and foreign records. store and id are in scope at the fork site (the waiter at start.c:1112 and the broker call at 1158-1159 already use them), and hold_mark_run_finished (record.c:972-1033) is pure fd work on absolute store paths, safe after setsid and after apply_child_env mutates the supervisor's environment (store->record_dir is a struct copy, no getenv). No test pins the fabricated 'Exited (0)' — the suite's Exited(N) assertions (test_hold.sh:640,1993,2715,2806,3303,3316) all go through the waiter's real mark. But the sketch has a real defect and two under-specified edges (amendments below), so accept only amended.
amendments: Dossier assumption 3 is wrong as sketched: 'status' is declared INSIDE the while loop (start.c:355); calling hold_mark_run_finished(status) before _exit(0) at 371 reads out-of-scope/uninitialized storage when g_restart_stop fires before the first waitpid completes. Hoist 'int status' with a 'bool have_status' flag; on the stop path with !have_status, skip the mark and let the record render 'Exited (?)' — never record a synthesized or garbage status.

Also mark on the fork-failure exit at start.c:342 (_exit(127)) with status 127<<8 when the record exists; otherwise that death path stays unwitnessed. Do NOT touch the pre-handshake _exit(127) paths (306-325): the parent sees the errno handshake and rolls the launch back before any record is written.

Gate condition in record.c must be: take the State.ExitCode fallback only when the already-parsed state (record.c:652-657) is 'exited' OR has_ended_at (646-651) is set — both parse before line 658 so it is implementable in place. Gating on has_ended_at alone is insufficient reasoning; gating on state alone is fine for the pinned foreign-record fixture (test_docker_shaped_record_fallback_reader, test_hold.sh:2977-3050, Status 'exited' + FinishedAt) but use the OR for belt-and-braces.

Do NOT change the writer: State.ExitCode must keep being emitted (0 while running, exactly like docker) — test_hold.sh:2956-2960 pins the full State key set, and inspect showing ExitCode 0 for running calls is docker's own behavior. Cut the self-deception at the loader only.
Do NOT touch the public-index loader (record.c:~873): test_public_root_index_list_reads_projected_state (test_hold.sh:2664-2698) pins that separate path.

Mirror the waiter's 50x100ms retry loop around the supervisor's mark (record may not exist yet when a fast-failing command exhausts on-failure:0 before the parent's record write at start.c:1408 — identical race the waiter already survives).

Harden test_log_viewer_integrated_chrome_help_and_info (test_hold.sh:955-984): it asserts 'VIEWING EXITED (0)' at :979 after a fixed 'sleep 0.3' at :966. Today a slow waiter is masked by the loader's laundering (interactive_view_exit_code, signal.c:499-511, sees the fabricated 0); with the gate that masking is gone and the fixed sleep becomes a flake. Replace the sleep with the existing record_ended_soon poll (test_hold.sh:519-530).

Accept and document the residual honest hole: 'hold end' on a restart call whose worker ignores TERM escalates to SIGKILL of the whole group after STOP_TIMEOUT_MS (signal.c:280,298); the supervisor dies unmarked and the call renders 'Exited (?)' where docker would say 137. That is the FDR trade working as designed — write it into the new test's comments and never assert an exact code on a forced-kill path.

play_verdicts.play: Foreground bare launch propagates the held process's exit code like docker run (start.c:1497-1517 tail branch), with a docker-parity-contract.md amendment

Define the algorithm precisely: after tail returns, reload the record (hold_load_record_by_id); if hold_eval_state is STATE_RUNNING (Ctrl-C interrupted the tail; the call stays held) return 0 immediately — never poll; if exited, poll up to 5s (matching the waiter's own 50x100ms retry) for has_exit_code; when marked, return exit_code (wait_status_exit_code already caps at 255, record.c:962-970); when still unmarked after the poll, print one stderr note ('hold: exit status unknown for call <id>') and return 1 — never fabricate 0 for an unwitnessed death, that would rebuild the exact lie this cluster removes.

--rm interplay (start.c:1505-1510): the child's code must win; keep the existing 'prune failure surfaces only when rc==0' shape so 'hold --rm cmd' matches docker run --rm (container code wins over cleanup errors).

Sequence dependency: land AFTER the FDR play. Foreground --restart calls (legal today; only tty is gated, start.c:823-825) anchor the record to the supervisor pid — without the supervisor's final frame the new rc path would hit the 5s-poll-then-rc-1 branch on every policy exhaustion.

Contract redline in the same commit: add to docker-parity-contract.md Flags section: 'A foreground launch exits with the held process's exit status once it ends (docker run parity). Interrupting the tail (Ctrl-C) leaves the call held and exits 0.' Also add 'Exited (?)' to the STATUS honest-extras list at docker-parity-contract.md:56-58 while the file is open — that phrase becomes user-visible via the FDR play and the contract should own it.
Explicitly out of scope (do not balloon): docker's 125/126/127 launch-failure codes — hold returns 1 for command-not-found today (start.c:1226-1239) and no finding licenses changing it.

implementation_risks: 1. Uninitialized 'status' in run_restart_supervisor: the variable is loop-local (start.c:355); the g_restart_stop-before-first-worker path reaches _exit(0) with no status ever set. Marking there records garbage. Must hoist with have_status and skip the mark when absent (renders honest 'Exited (?)').

2. Supervisor killed before the mark completes: 'hold end' escalates to SIGKILL of the whole group after STOP_TIMEOUT_MS=5000 (signal.c:280,298); a TERM-ignoring worker turns every end into a KILL that erases the supervisor mid-retry → 'Exited (?)' where docker says 137. By design under FDR, but a user-visible change — never assert exact codes on forced-kill test paths, and expect first-contact confusion ('?' read as data loss); the reboot case already renders 'Stale' (list.c:142-146) so '?' stays rare.

3. Foreground rc path hanging or mis-detaching: after tail, a reload+eval_state must gate the 5s exit-code poll — polling when the call is still RUNNING (Ctrl-C detach) would freeze the terminal for 5s; skipping the poll entirely would race the waiter's ~100ms mark and return rc 1 spuriously. The reload-once/eval/poll-only-if-exited order is load-bearing.

4. Mark-vs-record-write race in the supervisor: a command that exhausts on-failure:0 instantly can attempt the mark before the parent's hold_write_record_atomic (start.c:1408). The waiter has the identical race and survives via the 50x100ms retry; the supervisor must copy that idiom exactly, not a single-shot call.

5. -e PATH resolution regression: '-e PATH=/x cmd' currently finds /x/cmd via hold's polluted PATH (paths.c:93); after the fix it resolves against the invoker's PATH. Unpinned, but a silent behavior change — must be documented in the parity contract in the same commit, or a user bisecting will call it a break.

6. Cross-cluster surface collision: the console/broker cluster will also touch start.c (console branch, target_pid_pipe for console mode) and write final frames from broker_cleanup_and_exit. The FDR loader gate composes cleanly (marked broker records use the lowercase exit_code key), but coordinate start.c hunks — the restart-supervisor signature change (1070-1084) and any console-branch pid-anchoring change are adjacent in the same fork site.

7. Supervisor stderr noise lands in the call log: 'hold: restarting ... after exit status N' and any mark-failure message go through the dup2'd stderr pipe into the captured log (start.c:1062). A mark-retry-failure message would pollute user logs — keep the failure path silent or single-line.

8. Test-suite timing discipline: every new assertion must poll (record_ended_soon idiom, test_hold.sh:519-530) — the suite runs under a 25s per-test timeout (HOLD_TEST_TIMEOUT) with per-test isolated HOME (run_test/new_env, test_hold.sh:387-427); fixed sleeps are the existing flake vector the gate newly exposes (risk 1 in pinned conflicts).

9. macOS parity: no new platform seams — hold_mark_run_finished is POSIX fd work already exercised from an equally-detached waiter; the only conditional code near the touched sites is the pipe2/O_CLOEXEC fallback (start.c:960-1037) which is untouched. clear_process_environment's non-glibc branch (start.c:389-413) already runs on macOS for the supervisor path today.

Two holes in the play as sketched: (1) reserve-skip with no staleness rule turns a crashed start (SIGKILL leaves the reserve — only error paths unlink it, start.c:905-1050) into a PERMANENT sweep blind spot for that id's log/sock; (2) the adopted-call path (shell.c) creates log + console sock with NO reserve at all — shell_id_available (shell.c:318-340) never checks one and adopt never creates one — so the exact race the play claims to close stays open for 'hold on' adoptions, contradicting the play's own uniformity thesis. The unique-temp objection (silent last-write-wins replaces loud EEXIST) is honest and acceptable only with the flock fix as a NAMED deferral.
amendments: Reserve-skip must be age-gated: skip the orphan log/sock only when record_dir/.<id>.reserve exists AND its mtime is younger than 600s; an older reserve is a dead start's litter — unlink the reserve and proceed. (start's reserve->JSON window is sub-second; 600s is generous.) This keeps test_hold.sh:719-725 (test_orphan_log_cleanup, reg :4108) green: no reserve planted there.

Extend two-phase creation to the adopted path: in shell.c adopt_foreground_group, create .<id>.reserve (O_EXCL, mirroring reserve_hashed_run_id start.c:208-214) immediately after shell_hashed_adopt_id, before the log/sock creation at shell.c:469/:481; add the reserve check to shell_id_available; unlink it on every adopt failure path (record write already unlinks it — record.c:365 fires on ALL record writes). Without this, the play's thesis is half-true.

Corrupt-record accounting must NOT inflate stats->removed: the sweep prints one stdout line per purged call (list.c:711, 'Docker prune prints what it deletes') and the summary says 'purged N past calls' (:863) — folding corrupt files in makes count and lines disagree and the summary lie. Use a separate removed_corrupt counter, stderr line 'hold: warning: removing corrupt record %s' per file, summary suffix '; removed N corrupt record(s)', and print 'nothing to purge' only when both counters are 0. No test greps the summary strings today (verified), so wording is free — but stdout must stay machine-only per docker-parity-contract.md:66-69.

Keep the fchmod(fd, 0644) at record.c:405 when switching the public writer to hold_open_unique_temp — the mode argument is umask-subject exactly like the current open; dropping the fchmod would silently narrow public-entry permissions under restrictive umasks.

.tmp litter pass: in the record-dir and public-dir sweeps, collect dot-prefixed names ending '.tmp' only when mtime is older than 60s (protects a live writer's sub-ms window; unlinking a live tmp would make its rename fail loudly, but don't create that noise). The same rule collects legacy fixed-name .<id>.tmp tombs from existing v0.6.1 stores. Do NOT parse the embedded pid (the sketch's pid-liveness idea) — mtime is simpler and pid-in-name checking adds a parser for no gain.

NAMED DEFERRAL 'record-write serialization': per-id flock for load-modify-write (record.c:1015 rename/save vs reaper mark-finished lost updates). Unique temps convert today's loud EEXIST collision into silent last-write-wins; the commit message must state this trade explicitly. Do not attempt flock in this commit.
files_touched must add src/runtime/shell.c (adopted reserve); include/hold/core.h needs no change (declaration exists).

play_verdicts.play: Purge derives unlink targets from (store layout, call id); record path strings never followed for deletion
play_verdicts.reason: Verified end-to-end: the only two destructive consumers of record-stored paths are hold_prune_one_run (list.c:640-646) and the sweep record-branch (:700-706) — a full grep of unlink() sites shows every other unlink operates on paths the code itself constructed (start.c rollback paths, broker/socket own-path cleanup, computed orphan sweeps). hold_valid_record (validate.c:82-85) constrains nothing about paths; load accepts log_path verbatim (record.c:624-628) and console_sock with only a leading-'/' check (:677-679). Every writer constructs from layout: start.c:911 ('%s/%s.log') and :920 (hold_format_console_sock_path), shell.c:469/:481 identically, redial reuses the originally-constructed path (start.c:875-885); no --log-path flag exists (grep of cli_main.c returns nothing). So derivation is behavior-identical for every record hold ever wrote, and it removes the vulnerability class instead of adding a confinement check — genuinely stronger than the finding's own remedy_hint (prefix validation). The migration objection is actually WEAKER than stated: after a store move, today's code unlinks the stale stored path (ENOENT no-op) and LEAKS the real store-resident log until the next full sweep; derivation cleans the store-resident file and warns about the divergent stored path — a net improvement, not a regression. Two claims corrected: 'net negative lines in list.c' is false arithmetic (helper + two mismatch warnings ≈ +15-20 lines; sell it as authority reduction, not subtraction), and the closest pinned test — test_hold.sh:1893-1917 reads console_sock FROM THE RECORD (:1910) and asserts prune removes it (:1913) — stays green only because stored==computed for real calls; it must be re-run as the sentinel. Folding the play-2 idx fix into this commit is natural: unlink_log_index_for_log applied to the COMPUTED log path in both sites closes the FACT finding at list.c:701 without double-churning the same lines.
amendments: Compute the sock path via hold_format_console_sock_path (console.h:13) — the exact function every writer uses — instead of hand-rolling '%s/%s.sock'; runtime->console include direction is legal per scripts/lint_layers.sh. Compute the idx via hold_log_idx_path on the computed log.

Apply derivation in BOTH sites in this one commit (hold_prune_one_run :626-653 and sweep branch :699-707) and add the computed-idx unlink in the sweep branch here, closing ledger finding list.c:701 (FACT) — do not fix the idx against stored paths in the store commit and re-touch it here.

Mismatch warning: fire once per artifact per purge, stderr only ('hold: warning: record %s names a %s outside the store; left untouched: %s'), never stdout — docker-parity output discipline. When paths match (the universal case), zero new output.

Test fixture idiom: forge from a REAL ended call ('hold -d true', record_ended_soon, then sed log_path/console_sock in the record) — NOT a synthetic pid-12345 record, whose eval_state is nondeterministic when pid 12345 happens to be live on the test host.
SPEC.md gets exactly one added line under Safety invariants: 'Purge removes only store-resident artifacts derived from the call id; paths stored in records are never followed for deletion.'

NAMED FOLLOW-UPS (not this commit): (a) redial append still honors stored log_path (call.c ~:115-119 -> open_log_append_no_symlink) — the trust inversion is deliberately partial; (b) read-side consumers (hold logs/tail open r.log_path at signal.c:174/:845 with O_NOFOLLOW on the final component only) — same-privilege reads, lower severity, separate decision.

pinned_conflicts.what: PINS the leader-gone GROUP_LIVE fallback authorizing a signal with a recorded-but-uncompared token: 'hold -d bash -c "sleep 60 & exit 0"' must list as Up and 'hold end' must exit 0. Directly falsifies play 1's assumption #2 ('no existing test depends on the fallback succeeding'). The min-starttime-floor amendment keeps it green (sleep starts at/after the leader; a zombie leader's starttime equals the recorded value so strict-< passes); the finding's alternative remedy 'refuse leaderless groups without an override' is REJECTED by this pin.

Pins rc 2 plus stderr matching 'process group/session differs|cannot be signaled' for the live-leader tamper case. All new refusal messages introduced by play 1 must keep the 'cannot be signaled' phrasing to stay inside the message family this test establishes; it also constrains cleanup idiom (record .bak/restore, decoy teardown) for the new forged-record tests.
Pins rc 5 (not 2) for pgid<=1 tampering via the early guard at signal.c:96-99. The fail-closed rework must not reorder validation so this becomes rc 2.

Pins that bare 'hold prune' sweeps orphan .log files that have NO record and NO reserve. Play 2's reserve-skip must be conditional on reserve existence (and age), never a blanket behavior change to the orphan sweep.

Pins that bare 'hold prune' returns rc 0 AND removes a garbage record file (:661-662). Play 2's corrupt-removal warning/counting must not change the exit code or stop the removal; stderr is unredirected in this test so new warnings are safe, but the removal itself is load-bearing.

Reads console_sock out of the RECORD (:1910) and asserts targeted 'hold prune <id>' removes that exact path (:1913). Under play 3 the unlink target becomes the computed path — identical for real calls — so this test is the regression sentinel proving derivation is behavior-identical for hold-written records.

purge --force on a live saved call routes through hold_cmd_signal_action (list.c:930-937) and tolerates TERM->KILL timing with a 20x retry loop — but tolerates timing only, not refusals. Play 1 must introduce zero new refusal paths for healthy live calls or this test flakes into hard failure (rc 2 would leave the record present every attempt).

Fixtures still write 'requires_elevation' and 'alias' keys, confirming the ledger's store.md/projection-drift finding — that drift belongs to another cluster; this cluster's commits must NOT 'fix' the fixture or the projection in passing.

No test anywhere greps the purge summary strings ('nothing to purge', 'purged N past call'), no test pins the '.<id>.tmp' temp-name shape, and no test uses a TERM-trapping child — so the escalation branch signal.c:279-312 is currently unpinned entirely. dockerish :51-52 pins only that the .log.idx sidecar EXISTS for a live call, not its purge lifecycle.

implementation_risks: RANK 1 — Play 1 false refusal on adopted groups: a process older than the recorded leader can join the group via setpgid (pipeline/job-control edge in 'hold on' sessions), tripping the starttime floor and refusing a legitimate 'hold end' with rc 2. Mitigation: strict-< comparison (equal ticks pass), skip 'Z' members, refusal message names the cause; watch the adopted-shell tests (~test_hold.sh:3700s) and :1873 in CI. This is the one user-visible ACCESS regression candidate in the cluster.

RANK 2 — Play 2 reserve staleness threshold: too short re-opens the delete-nascent-call race for slow console/broker starts; too long delays post-crash cleanup by one sweep cycle. 600s is >100x the observed reserve->JSON window; document the constant beside STOP_TIMEOUT_MS-style config. A crashed start is otherwise a PERMANENT blind spot — the unamended sketch ships that bug.

RANK 3 — Play 2 unique temps convert concurrent record writers (rename/save at call.c:266/:335 vs reaper hold_mark_run_finished at record.c:1015) from loud EEXIST death/retry into silent last-write-wins. Data loss is unchanged (the loser's update was lost either way) but detectability regresses; the per-id flock is a named deferral and MUST be stated in the commit message.

RANK 4 — Play 1 macOS seam: hold_group_min_live_member_starttime needs a second KERN_PROC_ALL sysctl walk; starttime units differ per platform (Linux boot-relative ticks vs macOS usec wallclock, process.c:260-265) but are self-consistent within a boot; the boot-id gate at signal.c:107 only fires when r->has_boot — a boot-id-less record compared across boots yields garbage ticks, an exposure that already exists in the equality check at :117 and is not widened. Verify the macOS branch compiles zero-warning; CI is Linux-first.

RANK 5 — Play 1 changes the cross-uid recycled-pid outcome from misleading EPERM exit 3 to honest identity exit 2 (exe fail-closed path). No test pins exit 3 there (verified), but external scripts matching on 3 see a behavior change; changelog entry required.

RANK 6 — Shared-surface collision with other clusters: start.c (reserve/error paths), signal.c (validate), record.c (writers), broker/shell.c are all named surfaces in other clusters' likely changes. Land this cluster as commits 1->2->3 in one short window; commit 2 touches start.c NOT at all (reserve logic unchanged there) but shell.c adoption and record.c writers will conflict textually with any console-cluster work — rebase order matters.

RANK 7 — Play 3 migration stranding: records copied from an old HOME leave old-location files unswept (warned, never followed). Announced by the mismatch warning and consistent with no-legacy-shims; but the warning will repeat on every purge until the user removes the stale record — acceptable, documented.

RANK 8 — Purge summary/wording additions (corrupt counter, mismatch warnings) are unpinned today but become new de facto contracts the moment they ship; keep wording in the existing kept-string style (list.c:850-861) and pin the new strings in the new tests so future drift is caught.

c5-surface.cluster: C5 — Close the launch surface + honest help (intake/feedback): --cap-add/--cap-drop identity cut, --privileged remap deletion, exhaustive flag table + honest-rejection list in docker-parity-contract.md, usage-errors-to-stderr, ps/list help truthfulness, --name grammar hardening

play_verdicts.play: Play 1 — Close the launch-flag surface: execute the '--cap paths: delete' cut, delete the --privileged remap, amend docker-parity-contract.md with an exhaustive flag table + honest-rejection list
play_verdicts.reason: The deletion IS justified by internal evidence, conditionally. Verified: (1) every other element of cut row hold-on-identity.md:187 is executed — src/access/invocation.c is 62 lines of sudo-provenance detection only, no grants/elevation; (2) the normative (non-draft) docker-parity-contract.md:16-27 flag list omits both cap flags and --privileged while declaring 'Tests assert the shapes written here' — tests/test_hold.sh:2517-2602 assert shapes the contract does not write; (3) zero user-facing documentation: `./hold --help | grep -ci cap` = 0, no help_action topic in src/cli.c mentions them, --privileged appears only in docs/archive/; (4) --privileged's remap (cli_main.c:136-139 → requested_system at 342-343) is archive-era elevation vocabulary that lies to Docker muscle memory and produces an rc-3 error never naming the flag typed (cli_main.c:731); (5) capability management is container-substrate territory the identity doc ('more than nohup, less than systemd') and the honest-rejection precedent (-p/-v, contract lines 32-34) both exclude. The replacement spelling for --privileged's actual effect already exists and is what every test uses: the leading --system flag (cli_main.c:531-535, pinned at test_hold.sh:2772 '--system list' and 4193) and plain sudo/root (root already defaults to the system store, making --privileged a literal no-op for root). Zero tests and zero current docs use --privileged. BUT the play's own sketch is internally contradictory: step 2 says 'delete the --privileged branch' while the verify step demands `hold --privileged /bin/true` print an honest message and exit 5 — plain deletion yields 'cannot start "--privileged": command not found' rc 1 via the PATH fallthrough (start.c:832-836). Rejection branches, not silent deletion, are the contract-consistent mechanism. Also the sketch's 'emit empty CapAdd/CapDrop arrays' is wrong: record.c:135-141 currently emits null when the counts are zero, so hardcoding null (not []) keeps every cap-less record byte-identical across the cut and keeps the test_hold.sh:2960 key pin green with zero test edits. And --restart-delay must NOT enter the Docker-borrowed table — docker run has no such flag; the table's own header ('behave exactly like their docker run counterparts') would become false. Owner-ratification safety valve is mandatory since hold-on-identity.md:3 is still 'draft for redline': docs commit lands first and separately so reversing the decision is a one-paragraph edit plus one git revert.
amendments: --privileged and --cap-add/--cap-drop (all four spellings: split and = forms) get explicit rejection branches mirroring reject_publish_option (cli_main.c:282-286): exit 5, stderr message that names the flag typed. --privileged's message must point at the real spelling: 'run as root/sudo for the root-managed store, or pass --system'. --cap-*'s message: capabilities are a container-substrate concept; run under a user with the rights you need.

Record writer keeps the Config keys but hardcodes '"CapAdd": null' / '"CapDrop": null' (record.c:135-141 already emits null for zero counts) — byte-identical records for all cap-less launches, test_hold.sh:2960 and the 3024 fixture stay green untouched. 'Privileged' stays as-is: record.c:143 derives it from uid==0, never from the flag.

--restart-delay is documented in a NEW 'Hold-native launch flags' subsection of docker-parity-contract.md, not the Docker-borrowed table. That subsection must also name -f/--tail and --console (cli_main.c:484-493) plus --quiet/--system/-- — otherwise the 'closed set' claim is false the day it lands (these leading flags are currently doc-absent everywhere current).

The deletion (code + the three tests) is its own revertable commit, separate from and after the docs commit; CHANGELOG/release notes must flag BOTH the cut of working functionality AND the redial semantics change: a pre-cut record carrying CapDrop:['ALL'] now redials with full capabilities (loader lines record.c:762-774 deleted), which is a silent privilege WIDENING of a sandboxing recipe.
